channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Cómo dos directores de la generación Z formados en YouTube conquistaron el cine de terror
item.description: ‘Backrooms: sin salida’ y ‘Obsesión’ están reescribiendo las reglas de Hollywood. Dos críticos del Times analizan el fenómeno.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson and Jason Zinoman

item.title: The A.I. Proxy Fight Roiling the Race for a New York House Seat
item.description: Groups tied to OpenAI, Anthropic and other industry players have spent $16 million trying to pick the next member of Congress from Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

item.title: Brawls, Biting and Bliss in a City Craving a Knicks Championship
item.description: Decades of bottled-up hopes and frustrations have created a frenzy around the New York Knicks that has brought out the worst in some fans.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer and Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police officers stood guard as Game 4 of the N.B.A. finals drew a massive number of fans to Midtown Manhattan.

item.title: Knicks in Five? Please Don’t Tempt the Jinx Gods.
item.description: Some Knicks fans have predicted series sweeps. Others, conditioned by decades of failure, pay more heed to jinxes and superstitious rituals.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Knicks’ championship run has revealed a fundamental difference among some fans, with some unafraid of making boastful predictions and others recoiling at the jinx factor of doing so.

item.title: New York Primary Elections: What to Know and How to Vote
item.description: Several closely watched races are taking place in New York City and beyond. Nine days of early voting will precede Primary Day on June 23.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Among this year’s primary elections are several competitive races between Democrats in New York City.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 12, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: SpaceX Goes Public
item.description: SpaceX, Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite company is set to begin trading on Friday under the ticker symbol SPCX. Its valuation is set at $1.77 trillion and would put Mr. Musk, the world’s richest man, on track to become the first trillionaire in history.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:description: SpaceX will begin trading publicly on Friday under the ticker symbol SPCX.

item.title: Por qué es casi imposible fabricar un robot sin China
item.description: Aprovechando la industria de vehículos eléctricos del país, las empresas chinas están fabricando piezas para robots a una escala y a un precio que otros no pueden igualar.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin and Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: Por qué Spielberg sigue siendo el director más humano
item.description: El director más taquillero de la historia estrena su película número 35 y sigue convencido de que nada reemplaza la catarsis compartida de una sala llena.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris

media:content.media:credit: Paolo Pellegrin/Magnum, for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “For years, I was working out my mom and dad’s divorce through my stories,” Spielberg says.

item.title: Word of the Day: lien
item.description: This word has appeared in 29 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Late Night Prepares for Trump’s U.F.C. Birthday Fight at the White House
item.description: Jimmy Fallon called the president “the only 80-year-old yelling, ‘Get on my lawn!’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Reseña de ‘El día de la revelación’: Spielberg repasa sus éxitos cósmicos
item.description: En una trepidante fantasía de ciencia ficción, impregnada de fastuosidad y protagonizada por Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor y Colman Domingo, el cineasta nos lleva a todos a casa.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Cuando la IA resuelve en días lo que los matemáticos tardan años
item.description: Una empresa gastó 100.000 dólares en completar en cinco días una demostración que un equipo académico llevaba dos años construyendo. El episodio desató un pánico silencioso en los departamentos de matemáticas del mundo.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Barber

item.title: Ciclo de violencia en el Reino Unido: ataques de odio, agitación de la derecha y disturbios
item.description: Dos apuñalamientos han incitado protestas violentas en Inglaterra e Irlanda del Norte en los últimos 10 días, avivadas en internet por voces de la derecha. Es un patrón sombrío en el Reino Unido y en toda Europa.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Protestas al norte de Belfast, Irlanda del Norte, el miércoles en respuesta a un apuñalamiento ocurrido dos días antes.

item.title: ‘Operation Pushkin’: Paris Trial Puts Spotlight on Rare-Book Heists
item.description: One by one, valuable works by Russian masters like Pushkin and Gogol were disappearing from libraries across Europe. Now six defendants are being prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: A sculpture of Alexander Pushkin made of paper at a bookstore in St. Petersburg, Russia, this week.

item.title: U.S. Plan Is Said to Pull a Third of Fighter Jets It Provides NATO for Europe
item.description: The plan, outlined by officials and in a written document, provides rare clarity about the extent to which the Trump administration intends to reduce its commitment to NATO.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: An American F-16 jet at Andravida air base, Greece, last year.

item.title: Quote of the Day: A Sprint, a Leap and a Tap: Anunoby’s Moxie Turns Magical
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, June 12, 2026.

item.title: Corrections: June 12, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, June 12, 2026.

item.title: How to Eat Pizza with a Male Model
item.description: Order slices, fold them in half, flirt with male model, hope it makes your boyfriend jealous.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Krischer

item.title: A Teen Summer Romance and the Journal He Kept for Her
item.description: When Danit Hetsroni and Joshua Raikin met at summer camp, both had already navigated challenges that forced them to grow up quickly.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Katz

item.title: A Veterinarian, a Life Coach and a Highly Persuasive Pair of Donkeys
item.description: Dr. Evelyn Ivey knew Thomas Rotelli had the qualities she wanted in a mate, but watching how animals gravitated to him clinched it.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Katz

item.title: Matthew MacNabb and Laura Nolan of ‘Dancing with the Stars Ireland’ Marry
item.description: Laura Nolan developed a crush on Matthew MacNabb when he was on “Love Island UK.” Then they became dance partners on another show.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: ‘Darling, I Love You, but Give Me Park Avenue’
item.description: Lexie Riegelhaupt and Gabe Greenberg sang the “Green Acres” theme song as she left Park Avenue for his home in Carroll Gardens, Brooklyn.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

item.title: A Celebration and a ‘Love Ritual’ Shaped by Their Community
item.description: At the wedding of Danny Mefford and Yazmany Arboleda, guests made various contributions, including decorating the church and forming a choir.
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Signet Rings for Now
item.description: These days, the classic jewelry piece conveys personal style at least as much as it does lineage.
item.dc:creator: Angela Koh

item.title: Oil Falls, Stocks Rise as Trump Signals Iran Peace Deal Takes Shape
item.description: Oil prices retreated and stocks rallied after President Trump called off plans for another day of strikes on Iran, saying that a peace deal could be within reach.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Drones Flown Over North Korea Were Part of Martial Law Plot by Former South Korean President
item.description: A court ruled that Yoon Suk Yeol, the impeached former president, had sought to stir up instability to justify his bid for authoritarian rule in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun

media:content.media:description: Former President Yoon Suk Yeol of South Korea, center, arriving for a hearing to review his arrest warrant in Seoul in 2025.

item.title: China Has Arrested U.S. Scholar Who Studies Myanmar Politics
item.description: The arrest of U Min Zin, a graduate student at U.C. Berkeley and founder of a research group in Myanmar, took place soon after President Trump met with Xi Jinping in China.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong

item.title: The Four Suits You Should Have in Your Closet
item.description: Suits are back, and no matter the season or occasion, these styles are guaranteed to carry you through.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Montgomery

item.title: Princess Bha of Thailand, Second in Line to the Throne, Dies at 47
item.description: Ever since she collapsed in a park three and a half years ago, speculation has swirled in Thailand about who could become the next monarch.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee

media:content.media:description: Princess Bha of Thailand greeting royalists during a public appearance in Bangkok in 2020.

item.title: Review: ‘Romeo & Juliet’ Find Too Little Love in the Park
item.description: For the Public Theater’s Shakespeare in the Park, the director Saheem Ali presents a strangely low-energy version of the tragedy.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: From left, Ra’Mya Latiah Aikens as Juliet, Francis Jue as Friar Lawrence and Daniel Bravo Hernández as Romeo in the play “Romeo and Juliet” at the Delacorte Theater in New York.

item.title: Can Democrats Overcome G.O.P. Gerrymandering?
item.description: Our chief political analyst, Nate Cohn, who writes The Tilt newsletter, looks at the Republicans’ advantage in the House of Representatives after partisan redistricting. To win the House, how much of the popular vote would Democrats need to win?
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn, Laura Bult, June Kim, Edward Vega and Pierre Kattar

item.title: Trump Suspends Funding for Los Angeles Homeless Agency
item.description: The Trump administration cited misspending among the reasons for blocking funds to the Los Angeles Homeless Services Authority. Mayor Karen Bass warned that “people will lose their lives.”
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan

media:content.media:description: Scott Turner, the housing secretary, said his agency would not fund what he called the “corrupt failure” of Los Angeles’s homelessness efforts.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 12, 2026
item.description: Amanda Winters opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: James Hill for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Take your time with this Friday puzzle. There’s no rush.

item.title: Supreme Court Blocks Alabama From Executing Inmate Using Nitrogen Gas
item.description: The unsigned decision for now spares Jeffery Lee, a convicted murderer, and could lead to a broader fight over the relatively new execution method.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas and Abbie VanSickle

media:content.media:description: Jeffery Lee was scheduled for execution on Thursday before the ruling.

item.title: What to Know About Jay Clayton, Trump’s Pick for Intelligence Director
item.description: Mr. Clayton, the U.S. attorney in Manhattan and a former chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission, has been overseeing an office known for prominent cases.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jay Clayton, then serving as the U.S. attorney, at Gracie Mansion in New York last March. President Trump said he plans to choose him as the next director of national intelligence.

item.title: The Tug-of-War for Control of the House in 2026’s Midterm Elections
item.description: While public sentiment has drifted in Democrats’ favor in many of the midterm House races, Republicans have strong-armed a structural advantage: redrawing congressional maps.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Wu

item.title: ‘Obsession’ and the Caricature of the ‘Crazy Girlfriend’
item.description: Angelica Jade Bastién, a pop culture critic, joins Wesley Morris on “Cannonball” to talk about how women were portrayed in the hit horror movie “Obsession.”
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Eddie Costas, Austin Mitchell, John White and Felice Leon

item.title: National Mall Grass Vandalized with ’86 47’ Stains
item.description: The vandalism was the latest in a spate of apparent demonstrations involving the slang term, which the Trump administration says is a reference to assassinating the president.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: The National Park Service began a criminal investigation after discovering the numbers stained into the grass near the Washington Monument.

item.title: For a Knicks Reporter, Good Basketball Wasn’t Always So Easy to Find
item.description: In 2015, the team was so bad, The Times’s beat writer took a break to search for the game in its prettier forms.
item.dc:creator: Terence McGinley

media:content.media:credit: Michael Appleton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pickup at the McBurney Y.M.C.A. was a pick-me-up for a weary N.B.A. reporter.

item.title: Off-Duty Officer Indicted on a Charge of Attempted Murder Over Stolen Car
item.description: The officer fired two shots into another vehicle during the search for his car and struck a man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:description: Security footage of an off-duty police officer firing his gun into a vehicle. The officer has been suspended and a man is in critical condition.

item.title: Major League Baseball Will Question the Dodgers’s Doctor About Banned Drugs
item.description: The inquiry comes after a report that Dr. Neal ElAttrache, the physician for the team, supported the U.F.C. star Conor McGregor in using performance-enhancing drugs while recovering from an injury.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt

media:content.media:credit: The Yomiuri Shimbun, via Associated Press Images
media:content.media:description: Dr. Neal ElAttrache with Shohei Ohtani of the Los Angeles Dodgers during a game in Tokyo in 2025.

item.title: Kennedy Center Appeals Order to Remove Trump’s Name
item.description: One day before a deadline to take the president’s name off its facade, the arts institution appealed a federal judge’s ruling that also temporarily blocked it from closing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: Judge Christopher R. Cooper ruled that only Congress had the power to alter the name of the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts, which was dedicated to the president in a 1964 law.

item.title: Slayyyter on Her Viral Coachella Performaance.
item.description: Slayyyter, became one of 2026’s breakout pop stars with the release of her third album “Worst Girl in America” and her viral Coachella set.
item.dc:creator: Popcast
item.category: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

item.title: Copa Mundial 2026: partido de México en fotos
item.description: La ceremonia inaugural reunió a seguidores de México y visitantes de distintos países en una jornada que combinó celebraciones, espectáculos, protestas y una fuerte presencia policial.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Aficionados a las afueras del estadio de Ciudad de México antes del partido inaugural de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA el jueves.

item.title: Melania Trump Unveils New Investment Accounts for Foster Children
item.description: The program allows states to invest benefits they receive on behalf of foster children in their care.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green

media:content.media:description: Under the new program announced by Melania Trump, the Treasury Department will allow states to act as legal guardians to open accounts for foster youth in their care.

item.title: Some States Opt Out of Trump’s ‘Great American State Fair’
item.description: At least five have declined to participate, the latest sign that the national 250th birthday celebration has become a fragmented and partisan affair as the president seeks his imprint.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Sullivan

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Construction on the National Mall in preparation for the Great American State Fair.

item.title: Tornadoes Are Reported as Storms Sweep the Midwest
item.description: Chicago is in the bull’s-eye of severe weather for the second day in a row. Tornadoes were reported in Illinois, Indiana and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones

item.title: Whipsawed Between Fear and Relief, Iranians Hope for War’s End
item.description: In addition to concerns about their safety in the event of another all-out war, many Iranians worry about the country’s economy further collapsing if the conflict remains in limbo.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran on Monday.
